title: Brain-AI-101
description: Interactive neuroscience and AI learning platform that turns neurons, networks, filters, and feedback into guided web experiences.

title: DeepRetinaADRisk
description: Prediction of Alzheimer's disease risk factors from retinal images via deep learning.
citation: Seowung Leem, Yunchao Yang, Adam J. Woods, and Ruogu Fang. "Prediction of Alzheimer's disease risk factors from retinal images via deep learning: Development and validation of biologically relevant morphological associations in the UK Biobank" In Journal of Alzheimer's Disease, June 16, 2026. DOI: 10.1177/13872877261457650

title: tDCS J-map Outcome Prediction
description: Machine-learning pipeline for predicting tDCS response from MNI-registered current-density direction maps.

title: CROWN
description: Web platform for whole-head MRI segmentation and transcranial electrical stimulation modeling.
citation: Chintan K. Acharya, Dev Rakesh Patel, Skylar E. Stolte, Aprinda Indahlastari, and Ruogu Fang. "Comprehensive Reconstruction Of Whole-head Neuromodulation (CROWN): A Browser-Based Platform for Automated 11-Tissue MRI Segmentation and Individualized tDCS Current Density Modeling" In NYC Neuromodulation Conference, July 31-August 2, 2026, New York, NY, USA

title: Affective Batch Annotation
description: Batch-based affective image annotation tools for multimodal and LLM-based valence-arousal evaluation.

title: DOMINO CLI
description: Standalone CLI for processing NIfTI files with DOMINO and DOMINO++ models.
citation: Skylar Stolte, Kyle Volle, Aprinda Indahlastari, Alejandro Albizu, Adam Woods, Kevin Brink, Matthew Hale, and Ruogu Fang. "DOMINO: Domain-aware loss for deep learning calibration" In Software Impacts, Feb 2023. Skylar Stolte, Kyle Volle, Aprinda Indahlastari, Alejandro Albizu, Adam Woods, Kevin Brink, Matthew Hale, and Ruogu Fang. "DOMINO++: Domain-aware Loss Regularization for Deep Learning Generalizability" In MICCAI, 2023

title: GRACE CLI
description: Standalone CLI for processing NIfTI files with the GRACE whole-head segmentation model.
citation: Stolte, Skylar E. and Indahlastari, Aprinda and Chen, Jason and Albizu, Alejandro and Dunn, Ayden and Pederson, Samantha and See, Kyle B. and Woods, Adam J. and Fang, Ruogu. "Precise and Rapid Whole-Head Segmentation from Magnetic Resonance Images of Older Adults using Deep Learning" Imaging NeuroScience, March 2024

title: Less Is More
description: Real-time transdermal alcohol content artifact rejection and calibrated blood alcohol concentration estimation pipeline.

title: TRACE
description: Python tool for extracting ancestry-related information from biomedical literature using language models, regular expressions, and Cellosaurus metadata.
citation: Alison M. Veintimilla, Chintan K. Acharya, Connie J. Mulligan, Ruogu Fang, and Erika Moore. "TRACE: applying AI language models to extract ancestry information from curated biomedical literature" In Frontiers in Digital Health, September 2025

title: DeepDynaForecast
description: Phylogenetic-informed graph deep learning algorithm for forecasting epidemic transmission dynamics.
citation: Chaoyue Sun, Ruogu Fang, Marco Salemi, Mattia Prosperi, and Brittany Rife Magalis. "DeepDynaForecast: Phylogenetic-informed graph deep learning for epidemic transmission dynamic prediction" In PLOS Computational Biology, 2024

title: BrainSegFounder
description: This repository provides the official implementation of BrainFounder Stage 1 and 2 Pretraining and Fine-tuning methods presented in the paper: BrainFounder: Towards Brain Foundation Models for Neuroimage Analysis
citation: Joseph Cox, Peng Liu, Skylar E. Stolte, Yunchao Yang, Kang Liu, Kyle B. See, Huiwen Ju, and Ruogu Fang. "BrainFounder: Towards Brain Foundation Models for Neuroimage Analysis" In Medical Image Analysis, October 2024

title: tDCS-DT
description: This repository provides the official implementation of training and evaluation of the model as described in the following paper: Towards tDCS Digital Twins using Deep Learning-based Direct Estimation of Personalized Electrical Field Maps from T1-Weighted MRI
citation: Skylar E. Stolte, Aprinda Indahlastari, Alejandro Albizu, Adam J. Woods, and Ruogu Fang "Towards tDCS Digital Twins using Deep Learning-based Direct Estimation of Personalized Electrical Field Maps from T1-Weighted MRI" In MICCAI 2024

title: LAVA: Granular Neuron-Level Explainable AI for Alzheimer's Disease Assessment from Fundus Images
description: CodeLAVA is an XAI framework that aims to exploit neuron-level explanation as auxiliary information during learning process to make a high-resolution AD continuum prediction.
citation: Nooshin Yousefzadeh, Charlie Tran, Adolfo Ramirez-Zamora, Jinghua Chen, Ruogu Fang, My T. Thai. "LAVA: Granular Neuron-Level Explainable AI for Alzheimer's Disease Assessment from Fundus Images" In Nature Scientific Reports, April 2024.

title: GRACE
description: This repository provides the official implementation of GRACE: General, Rapid, And Comprehensive whole-hEad tissue segmentation
citation: Stolte, Skylar E. and Indahlastari, Aprinda and Chen, Jason and Albizu, Alejandro and Dunn, Ayden and Pederson, Samantha and See, Kyle B. and Woods, Adam J. and Fang, Ruogu. "Precise and Rapid Whole-Head Segmentation from Magnetic Resonance Images of Older Adults using Deep Learning" Imaging NeuroScience, March 2024

title: RetinaPD
description: CodeThe purpose of this project is for the binary classification of Parkinson's Disease from UK Biobank Fundus Imaging.
citation: Charlie Tran, Kai Shen, Kang Liu, Akshay Ashok, Adolfo Ramirez-Zamora Jinghua Chen, Yulin Li, Ruogu Fang. "Deep Learning Predicts Prevalent and Incident Parkinson’s Disease From UK Biobank Fundus Imaging" In Nature Scientific Reports, 2024.

title: DOMINO++: Domain-aware Loss Regularization for Deep Learning Generalizability
description: Code in this work, we propose DOMINO++, a dual-guidance and dynamic domain-aware loss regularization focused on OOD generalizability.
citation: Skylar E. Stolte, Kyle Volle, Aprinda Indahlastari, Alejandro Albizu, Adam J. Woods, Kevin Brink, Matthew Hale, and Ruogu Fang. "DOMINO++: Domain-aware Loss Regularization for Deep Learning Generalizability" In MICCAI, 2023

title: DOMINO: Domain-aware Model Calibration in Medical Image Segmentation
description: CodeDOMINO is an open-source package for domain-aware model calibration that leverages the semantic confusability and hierarchical similarity between class labels in multi-class classification/segmentation to improve model calibration while not sacrificing or even improving model accuracy.
citation: Stolte, Skylar E., Kyle Volle, Aprinda Indahlastari, Alejandro Albizu, Adam J. Woods, Kevin Brink, Matthew Hale, and Ruogu Fang. "DOMINO: Domain-Aware Model Calibration in Medical Image Segmentation." In International Conference on Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention, pp. 454-463. Springer, Cham, 2022.

title: EEGAILab
description: CodeToolbox for analyzing and visualizing dipole sources from resting electroencephalography data.

title: CADA
description: CodeMulti-scale Collaborative Adversarial Domain Adaptation for Unsupervised Optic Disc and Cup Segmentation

title: Domain Shift
description: CodeAdversarial Discriminative Adaptation and Ensembling Collaborative Learning for Domain Shift in Unsupervised Optic Disc and Cup Segmentation

title: EvoNet
description: CodeDeep evolutionary networks with expedited genetic algorithms for medical image denoising

title: CT Perfusion Toolbox
description: CodeToolbox for CT perfusion image processing and computation, including image loading, preprocessing, reconstruction, quantification, and example code.

title: Tensor Total Variation
description: CodeToolbox for Tensor Total Variation quantfication of CT perfusion parameters in low-dose CT perfusion.

title: Sparse High-Dose Induced Patlak Model for Permeability
description: CodeSparse high-dose induced Patlak model for Permeability quantification in low-dose CT perfusion.

title: Sparse Perfusion Deconvolution Toolbox
description: CodeSparse perfusion deconvolution toolbox using dictionary learning and sparse coding for low-dose CT perfusion parameters quantification.

title: Kinship Classification Dataset
description: DataFamily101 Dataset: first large-scale dataset of families across several generations. It contains 101 different family with distinct family names, including 206 nuclear families, 607 individuals, with 14,816 images.

title: Kinship Verification Dataset
description: DataCornell Kinship Verification Dataset (143 pairs of parents and children)
